description: Canonical schema definitions for all diagnostics and state files produced by the BOX runtime. Each entry defines the format, required fields, and serialization contract for a specific state file.

dependency_graph_diagnostics.json.description: One JSON object per line, appended by persistGraphDiagnostics() on every dependency graph resolution. Each line is independently parseable — no cross-line references.

requiredFields.persistedAt: string — ISO-8601 timestamp set at write time
requiredFields.status: string — one of GRAPH_STATUS enum values (ok, cycle_detected, invalid_input, degraded)
requiredFields.reasonCode: string — one of GRAPH_REASON enum values
requiredFields.resolvedAt: string — ISO-8601 timestamp from resolveDependencyGraph result
requiredFields.totalTasks: number — count of tasks in the resolved graph
requiredFields.waves: array — wave assignment objects with taskIds and gates
requiredFields.cycles: array — cycle path arrays detected during topological sort

optionalFields.parallelTasks: number — count of tasks in waves with >1 task
optionalFields.serializedTasks: number — count of tasks serialized by conflict/dep constraints
optionalFields.errorMessage: string | null — error message for invalid_input or cycle_detected status

dependency_graph_diagnostics.json.parseContract: Each line must be parseable with JSON.parse() independently. Readers must iterate lines and parse each individually, skipping empty lines.

intervention_optimizer_log.json.description: Single JSON object updated atomically by the intervention optimizer. Not NDJSON — the entire file is a single object with an entries array.

requiredFields.updatedAt: string — ISO-8601 timestamp of last write
requiredFields.entries: array — intervention log entries, each representing one optimization decision

intervention_optimizer_log.json.parseContract: The entire file is a single valid JSON object. Read with JSON.parse(fileContents). Use readJson/writeJson for atomic updates to prevent corruption.

drift_backlog.json.description: One JSON object per line, appended by persistDriftBacklog() on each architecture drift scan. Each line is an independently parseable backlog entry.

requiredFields.persistedAt: string — ISO-8601 timestamp set at write time
requiredFields.source: string — caller label identifying which component persisted this entry
requiredFields.staleCount: number — count of stale file references found in this scan
requiredFields.deprecatedTokenCount: number — count of deprecated token usages found in this scan
requiredFields.debtTaskCount: number — count of PlannerDebtTask objects in debtTasks array
requiredFields.debtTasks: array — PlannerDebtTask objects with taskId, task, priority, confidence, type, source, debtClass, createdAt

drift_backlog.json.parseContract: Each line must be parseable with JSON.parse() independently. Readers must iterate lines and parse each individually, skipping empty lines. Use appendFile for writes, never overwrite.

carry_forward_backlog.json.description: Single JSON object listing machine-checkable closure records for recurring verification debt items. Static reference document — not appended at runtime.

requiredFields.description: string — human-readable description of the backlog
requiredFields.items: array — closure record objects with id, title, status, closureEvidence, closedBy, closedAt

carry_forward_backlog.json.parseContract: The entire file is a single valid JSON object. Read with JSON.parse(fileContents).
